exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.
instructions: Set up a flat bench inside a rack so the barbell sits directly above your eyes when you are lying down.

Lie supine on the bench with your head, upper back and glutes in contact with the bench, feet flat on the floor and a small natural arch in the lower back; retract and depress your shoulder blades to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the barbell with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width; wrists should be stacked over the elbows and forearms vertical when the bar is over the chest.
Unrack the bar by straightening the arms and moving the bar out over the chest while keeping the scapulae stable; lock the elbows briefly before starting the set.
Inhale and lower the bar in a controlled manner to the mid-chest/sternum (around nipple level), keeping the elbows at about a 45-degree angle from the torso to protect the shoulders.
Allow the bar to touch or come very close to the chest without bouncing; maintain tension in the pectorals and keep the feet and glutes braced.
Exhale and press the bar upward explosively while keeping the bar path slightly arced toward the hips until the arms are fully extended but not hyperextended.
Pause briefly at the top with the shoulders stable, then begin the next rep. Maintain consistent form and breathing throughout the set.
Use a spotter or safety pins in the rack for heavy sets, and avoid flaring the elbows excessively or letting the lower back lift off the bench.
exercises.description: The barbell bench press is a compound pressing movement performed lying supine on a flat bench that primarily develops the pectorals while also recruiting the triceps and anterior delts. It involves lowering a loaded barbell to the chest and pressing it back up to full arm extension with controlled technique.

Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Focus on explosive concentric movements.
Take full rest between sets for neural recovery.

exercises.name: Cable Pulldown (pro lat bar)
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate weight and grasp the pro-lat bar with a wide, pronated (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit on the pulldown bench with knees secured under the thigh pads and feet flat on the floor (seated, knees braced).
Select an appropriate weight and grasp the pro-lat bar with a wide, pronated (palms facing away) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Begin tall with a neutral spine and a slight lean back from the hips (not from the lower back), chest lifted and eyes forward.
Inhale, then exhale and initiate the movement by pulling the shoulder blades down and together (scapular retraction) to engage the lats.
Pull the bar down in a controlled arc toward the upper chest (sternal area), leading with the elbows and keeping the wrists neutral.
Pause briefly at the bottom and squeeze the lats and upper back without shrugging the shoulders.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the start position with straight arms, fully extending the shoulders while maintaining a stable torso.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, avoiding excessive torso lean or momentum; keep movement controlled and driven by the lats.
exercises.description: Seated cable pulldown using a pro-lat bar performed from a tall seated position, pulling the bar down to the upper chest to target the latissimus dorsi and upper back. Focus is on scapular retraction and controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Decline Fly
exercises.notes: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet in the foot pads so you can remain stable while supine. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Set a decline bench to a moderate angle and secure your feet in the foot pads so you can remain stable while supine.
Pick two light-to-moderate dumbbells and sit down; then lie back so your head is lower than your hips (decline position) and your feet are anchored.
Start with the dumbbells held directly above your lower chest, palms facing each other (neutral grip), arms almost straight but with a slight, fixed bend in the elbows.
Brace your core and keep your shoulder blades pulled slightly together to stabilize the shoulder girdle.

Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells out to your sides in a controlled wide arc, keeping the same slight elbow bend; aim to lower until you feel a stretch in the lower chest but do not let the elbows drop behind the plane of your torso.
Pause briefly at the bottom without relaxing the shoulders, maintaining tension in the pectorals and serratus anterior.
Exhale and drive the dumbbells back up in the same arc using your chest, bringing them to meet above your lower sternum; avoid using momentum or locking the elbows at the top.
Perform each rep with a controlled tempo (2–4 seconds descent, 1 second concentric) and keep the wrists neutral; reduce weight if you feel shoulder pain.
When finished, bring the dumbbells safely to your thighs and sit up slowly to avoid dizziness from the decline position.
exercises.description: Lying supine on a decline bench, the dumbbell decline fly isolates the lower portion of the pectorals by moving the arms in a wide arc with a fixed elbow angle. It stretches the chest on the descent and contracts the chest to bring the weights together over the sternum.

exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced. Train to muscular failure.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.

Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Every working set should reach true muscular failure.
Use slow, controlled tempo throughout the movement.
Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.

exercises.name: Barbell Feet Flat Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
instructions: Set the bench inside a rack so the bar sits at arm height when you lie down; load the bar and secure collars or have a spotter.
Lie supine on the flat bench with your eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and planted for stability, and a slight natural arch in the lower back.
Retract and depress your shoulder blades (pinch them together and press them into the bench) to create a stable chest platform.
Grip the bar with a full grip slightly wider than shoulder width, wrists stacked over forearms and elbows under the bar.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar into the start position directly above your shoulders.
Inhale, then lower the bar in a controlled manner to mid-chest / nipple line, keeping elbows at about a 45° angle to the torso and maintaining scapular retraction.
Briefly pause with the bar lightly contacting the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward along a slightly arced path back to full arm extension.
Lock out the elbows at the top without overextending, keep the feet planted and core braced throughout the set.
After the final rep, carefully rack the bar onto the supports or have your spotter assist; use a spotter for heavy loads and maintain strict control on every rep.
exercises.description: Barbell feet-flat flat bench press is a supine pressing exercise that primarily works the pectorals by pressing a loaded barbell from the chest to full arm extension. Keeping the feet flat provides a stable base while the triceps and delts assist in elbow extension and shoulder stabilization.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Wide grip Row
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the bench/seat with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent; grasp the wide rowing bar with a wide, overhand grip.
instructions: Sit upright on the bench/seat with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent; grasp the wide rowing bar with a wide, overhand grip.
Start with arms extended, torso upright and chest up, maintaining a neutral spine and slight forward lean from the hips.
Initiate the movement by pulling the shoulder blades back and down (scapular retraction), then drive the elbows out and back to bring the bar toward the upper abdomen/lower chest.
Squeeze the upper back and hold the contracted position for 1 second, keeping the neck neutral and avoiding excessive torso lean or momentum.
Slowly return the bar to the start by extending the arms under control while maintaining an engaged core and neutral spine; inhale on the return, exhale during the pull.
Repeat for desired reps, keeping movements smooth and avoiding jerking or using the lower back to complete the pull.
exercises.description: Seated cable wide-grip rows train the upper back by pulling a wide handle toward the torso, emphasizing the mid-back and rear shoulder muscles while minimizing lower-back involvement. Use controlled scapular retraction and elbow drive to maximize upper-back engagement.

exercises.name: Chest Dip (on dip pull-up cage)
exercises.notes: Grip the parallel bars of the dip/pull-up cage with hands shoulder-width or slightly wider and arms fully extended; support your body weight with feet off the floor (knees can be bent or ankles crossed).
instructions: Grip the parallel bars of the dip/pull-up cage with hands shoulder-width or slightly wider and arms fully extended; support your body weight with feet off the floor (knees can be bent or ankles crossed).
Lean your torso forward ~20–30 degrees from vertical and retract your shoulder blades slightly to place emphasis on the pectorals; keep a neutral neck and core braced.
Inhale and slowly lower your body by bending the elbows and allowing them to flare mildly outward, keeping the forward lean so your chest moves toward the bars.
Descend until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or your chest reaches the level of the bars (stop earlier if you feel shoulder pain); maintain control—do not drop suddenly.
Exhale and press through your palms to straighten the elbows and return to the start position with arms extended, keeping the forward lean and core engaged.
Perform reps with controlled tempo (e.g., 2–3 seconds down, 1 second pause, explosive up); avoid locking shoulders at the top and do not hyperextend the elbows.
If the movement is too difficult, use an assisted dip machine, resistance band support, or place feet on a low platform until you can perform unassisted reps with proper form.
exercises.description: A chest-focused dip performed on parallel bars/dip station with a forward lean to emphasize the pectorals while still engaging the triceps and delts. Keep legs off the floor and descend in a controlled arc until chest is level with the hands, then press back up.

exercises.name: Pull-up
exercises.notes: Select an appropriate assistance (counterweight) on the assisted pull-up/dip machine and lower the knee pad to a comfortable height.
instructions: Select an appropriate assistance (counterweight) on the assisted pull-up/dip machine and lower the knee pad to a comfortable height.
Stand facing the bar and grasp it with a pronated (overhand) grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Mount the machine and kneel on the pad with knees supported and feet either crossed or tucked behind; allow the arms to fully extend so your body hangs straight.
Brace your core, keep your head neutral, and pull your shoulder blades down and back to initiate the movement.
Exhale and pull yourself upward by driving your elbows down and back, bringing your chin or upper chest toward the bar while squeezing the lats and upper back.
Pause briefly at the top with the scapulae retracted and avoid excessive shrugging of the delts.
Inhale and slowly lower yourself under control until your arms are fully extended and shoulders are not shrugged.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions; reduce assistance (use a lighter counterweight) as you get stronger to increase difficulty.
exercises.description: Assisted pull-up on a counterweight machine where the user kneels on the support pad and pulls their body up to the bar; this exercise primarily targets the lats while also working the pulling muscles of the arms and upper back.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Standing Military Press
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent (soft), grip the barbell just wider than shoulder-width with palms facing forward.
Hold the bar at upper-chest / front-shoulder level (resting on front delts / clavicle area), elbows pointed slightly forward and directly under the wrists.
Brace your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back to stabilize the spine; keep a neutral head and avoid excessive arching of the lower back.
Inhale, then press the barbell straight up overhead by extending the elbows and driving through the delts; move the bar slightly back only enough to end with it stacked over mid-foot at full lockout.
Exhale at the top with arms fully extended but avoid hyperextending the elbows and lower back; keep the ribcage down and core tight.
Lower the bar under control back to the starting position at the upper chest, keeping wrists neutral and elbows tracking under the bar.
Repeat for desired reps. Use a spotter or lighter weight if not using a rack and maintain strict form to protect shoulders and spine.
exercises.description: A standing barbell press performed from the front shoulder/chest area to overhead, primarily working the shoulder delts while pressing the weight up in a vertical path. The lift requires core stabilization and arm extension to lock out overhead.

exercises.name: Barbell Clean and Press
exercises.notes: Start standing with feet about hip-width to slightly wider, barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar just outside the knees with a double overhand or hook grip and chest up.
instructions: Start standing with feet about hip-width to slightly wider, barbell over mid-foot; grip the bar just outside the knees with a double overhand or hook grip and chest up.
Hinge at the hips and bend the knees so your hips are low, back flat, shoulders slightly in front of the bar and eyes forward (front of a squat rack/sightline).
Initiate the lift by driving through the heels and extending the hips and knees explosively while keeping the bar close to your body; shrug forcefully at full extension.

As you shrug, pull the elbows high and outside to accelerate the bar upward and quickly drop under the bar into a partial front-rack squat to 'catch' the bar on your shoulders (front rack position) with elbows high.
Stand up tall from the catch, fully extending the hips and knees to a stable upright front-rack position with the bar resting on the shoulders/clavicles and elbows forward.

From the front-rack, press the bar overhead by driving through the legs slightly (if using a push press) or strictly extend the arms (strict press), finishing with arms locked overhead and head slightly forward under the bar.

Lower the bar back to the front-rack in a controlled manner, then return the bar to the floor by hinging at the hips and bending the knees (reset) or carefully drop into a controlled descent if using appropriate technique and environment.
Maintain a braced core and neutral spine throughout, keep the bar path close to the body, and breathe/exhale during the press portion; reset between reps if necessary to preserve form.
exercises.description: A compound Olympic-style lift that combines a barbell clean (explosive hip extension and pull to bring the bar to the front rack) with a strict or push overhead press to finish. Trains shoulder pressing strength while recruiting legs, posterior chain, upper back and core for power and stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Lie back (supine) on the bench while bringing the dumbbells to chest level; plant your feet flat on the floor and create a slight natural arch in your lower back.
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at your thighs.

Lie back (supine) on the bench while bringing the dumbbells to chest level; plant your feet flat on the floor and create a slight natural arch in your lower back. Retract your shoulder blades and keep them contacting the bench.
Start with the dumbbells at mid-chest, elbows bent about 90 degrees and wrists neutral (dumbbell handles aligned with forearms).
Exhale and press both dumbbells upward in a controlled arc until your arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended; keep wrists stacked over elbows.

Inhale and lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position, maintaining control and stopping when the dumbbells reach chest level or your elbows form ~90 degrees. Keep elbows at roughly a 30–45° angle from your torso to protect the shoulders.
Keep your core braced and feet firmly planted for stability throughout each rep; avoid excessive flaring of the ribs or letting the shoulders lift off the bench.
Use a spotter for heavy loads and choose a weight that allows controlled full-range reps with good form.
exercises.description: Flat dumbbell bench press performed lying supine on a bench, pressing two dumbbells upward from chest level to develop the pectorals; the movement also recruits the anterior delts and triceps for stability and lockout.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Row with V-bar
exercises.notes: Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
instructions: Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with feet braced against the footplate and knees slightly bent.
Attach the V-bar to the low pulley and grasp it with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Sit upright with a neutral spine and chest lifted.
Start with your arms extended and shoulders relaxed forward — feel a slight stretch in the lats.
Exhale and initiate the movement by driving your elbows straight back, keeping them close to your sides.
Retract and squeeze the shoulder blades together and pull the V-bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen; avoid excessive torso lean or using momentum.
Hold and squeeze the contracted position for 1 second, focusing on the lats and upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward under control until the lats are stretched and you return to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining a neutral spine and controlled tempo throughout.
exercises.description: Seated cable row with a V-bar is a horizontal pulling exercise that emphasizes the lats and middle back by pulling the handle toward the torso while seated. It uses a neutral grip to allow a strong elbow-driven pull and a full contraction of the back muscles.

exercises.name: Cable Front Seated Row
exercises.notes: Adjust the cable pulley to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the cable pulley to chest height and select an appropriate weight.
Sit on the bench facing the machine with your chest pressed against the vertical support pad, feet flat on the floor and knees bent at about 90°.
Reach forward and grasp the handle with a neutral or overhand grip, arms fully extended and shoulders relaxed.
Keep your torso upright and core braced; maintain contact with the chest pad throughout the movement.
Exhale and pull the handle straight back by driving your elbows toward your ribcage, leading with the elbows and squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Pause briefly at the end range when your hands reach your torso and you feel a full contraction across the upper back.
Inhale and slowly extend your arms forward in a controlled manner until they are nearly straight, resisting the weight on the return.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding trunk rotation, excessive trunk lean, or shrugging the shoulders; keep the motion controlled and driven by the back, not the arms.
exercises.description: A seated cable row variation performed facing the machine with chest supported by a pad, pulling a single handle straight toward the torso to work the mid/upper back. Emphasis is on scapular retraction and elbow drive to target the upper back and lats.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Cable Middle Fly
exercises.notes: Set both cable pulleys to about mid-chest height and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Set both cable pulleys to about mid-chest height and select an appropriate weight.
Stand centered between the towers in a staggered stance (one foot slightly forward) with a slight bend in the knees and a neutral spine.
Grasp the handles with a neutral or palms-facing grip and extend your arms out to the sides so there is tension on the cables; keep a small, constant bend in the elbows.
Retract your shoulder blades and keep the shoulders down; brace your core to prevent torso movement.
Exhale and bring the handles together in front of your chest in a wide arc, leading with the elbows and squeezing the pectorals at the end range.
Inhale and slowly reverse the motion with control, allowing the arms to return to the starting position until you feel a gentle stretch in the chest.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, avoiding torso leaning, shoulder shrugging, or locking the elbows.
exercises.description: Standing bilateral cable fly performed with the pulleys set at mid-chest height; the exercise horizontally adducts the arms in a controlled arc to load and contract the pectorals. A staggered stance and slight elbow bend are used to stabilize and protect the joints while the chest performs the work.

exercises.name: Lever Outdoor Seated Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seat with feet flat on the ground and knees braced under any available pads (or firmly planted).
instructions: Sit upright on the seat with feet flat on the ground and knees braced under any available pads (or firmly planted). Adjust seat height so your arms can fully extend overhead to grasp the bar.
Reach up and grasp the bar with a wide, overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, thumbs wrapped around the bar.
Engage your core, pull your shoulder blades down and back (depress and retract) to take tension in the lats before initiating the movement.
Exhale and pull the bar straight down toward your upper chest by driving your elbows down and back; keep the torso relatively upright with only a slight lean back.
Squeeze the lats and upper-back muscles at the bottom of the movement, keeping the shoulders down and avoiding shrugging.
Inhale and slowly allow the bar to return to the fully extended overhead start position with controlled resistance, keeping tension in the lats throughout.
Repeat for the desired reps, avoiding momentum or excessive torso swing — keep the movement smooth and controlled.
exercises.description: A seated cable/lever pulldown performed on an outdoor station that targets the latissimus dorsi by pulling a bar from overhead down toward the upper chest. Performed seated with a controlled vertical pull, it also stresses the arms and upper-back stabilizers.

exercises.name: Barbell Pendlay Row
exercises.notes: Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over mid-foot; bend at the hips and knees to reach the bar so your torso is roughly parallel to the floor (hip hinge), knees slightly bent.
Grip the bar with a double overhand or mixed grip about shoulder-width apart, keep a neutral spine and eyes looking a few feet ahead on the floor.
Brace your core, pull your shoulder blades down and back, then explosively row the bar in a straight line to your lower chest/upper abdomen, driving your elbows back.
Squeeze your upper back at the top for 1 second while keeping the torso stable (no rowing with the hips or excessive torso rotation).
Lower the bar under control all the way back to the floor so it comes to a complete stop, reset any slack, then repeat for the desired reps.
Maintain a flat back and braced core throughout; if your lower back rounds or you must use momentum, reduce weight or raise the bar height.
exercises.description: The Barbell Pendlay Row is a strict horizontal barbell row performed from a dead stop on the floor each rep, emphasizing the upper back and lats while minimizing torso momentum. It uses a hip hinge with a flat back and pulls the bar to the lower chest/upper abdomen before returning it to the floor.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Every working set should reach true muscular failure.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced to maintain a neutral spine.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward (or slightly rotated) and elbows bent roughly 90 degrees, wrists stacked over elbows.
Inhale, then exhale as you press both dumbbells upward in a controlled path until your arms are nearly extended overhead; avoid fully locking the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top while keeping the shoulders down and chest lifted (do not shrug excessively).
Inhale as you slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting shoulder-height position under control.
Keep the back pressed against the bench and avoid excessive lower-back arching or leaning; repeat for desired reps.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell shoulder press performed from a back-supported bench to drive the weights overhead, primarily working the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with triceps and upper traps assisting. The movement emphasizes vertical pressing strength and shoulder stability.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Upright Row
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (neutral spine).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart, knees soft and core braced (neutral spine).
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand (pronated) grip, arms extended down in front of you and weights close to your thighs.
Initiate the movement by driving your elbows upward and outward, keeping the dumbbells close to the body and leading with the elbows.
Raise the dumbbells until your elbows reach roughly chest/shoulder height or just below chin level, keeping wrists relatively neutral and palms facing your body or slightly inward.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulders engaged but avoid excessive shrugging or leaning back.
Lower the dumbbells slowly and under control back to the starting position, fully extending the arms without locking the elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining an upright standing position, steady breathing, and avoiding momentum or swinging.
exercises.description: A standing dumbbell upright row where you pull the weights vertically toward your upper chest, emphasizing the shoulder (deltoid) muscles with assistance from the trapezius, biceps, and forearms. Perform with a controlled motion and elbows leading to keep tension on the delts.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Close Grip Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Pick up two dumbbells and hold them close together at chest/shoulder height with a neutral or slightly pronated grip so the heads are nearly touching and elbows point down and slightly forward.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides (not flaring out) and maintain an upright torso throughout the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are almost fully extended, stopping short of locking the elbows.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position at shoulder height under control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining tight core and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
Common cues: keep dumbbell heads close together, drive through the triceps rather than pushing with the chest, and avoid flaring the elbows outward.
exercises.description: Seated dumbbell close-grip press is a pressing movement performed sitting with the dumbbells held close together at shoulder level; it emphasizes the triceps while also engaging the front delts and inner chest. Press the weights upward in a controlled motion, keeping the dumbbells close to each other and elbows tucked.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Maintain a steady rhythm throughout each set.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Arnold Press
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent at ~90°, and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing your torso and elbows bent.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent at ~90°, and core braced; hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with palms facing your torso and elbows bent.
Keep your chest up and back neutral (avoid excessive lower-back arch) and prepare to press by rotating your wrists outward so your palms will face forward at the top of the movement.
Exhale and press the dumbbells overhead while simultaneously rotating your palms until the arms are extended overhead and palms face forward; do not lock or hyperextend the elbows.
Pause briefly at the top with the weights controlled overhead, maintaining tension in the delts and triceps.
Inhale and lower the dumbbells back down in a controlled manner while rotating the wrists back to the starting position with palms facing your torso and elbows returning to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping movement smooth and avoiding using leg drive or excessive torso lean to lift the weights.
exercises.description: A seated rotational dumbbell press that works the shoulder muscles through a palms-in to palms-forward rotation, emphasizing the delts while also engaging the triceps and upper back. Performed seated to limit torso drive and increase shoulder isolation.
